title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. It can lead to data encryption, service disruption, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread propagation and supply chain attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strong authentication, access controls, and monitoring for unauthorized changes.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline or in a secure, immutable environment.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and monitor for indicators of compromise (IoCs) related to the ransomware campaign.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk; however, widespread adoption without proper vetting could lead to potential misuse, false positives, or reliance on incomplete vulnerability databases. Additionally, attackers could exploit poorly implemented or insecure versions of the tool.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before integrating it into workflows.
Ensure the tool is used in conjunction with other security measures and not as the sole method of vulnerability management.
Monitor for updates or reports of vulnerabilities in the tool itself and apply patches promptly.

title: The RockPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in all versions up to,...
description: The RockPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Missing Authorization in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.17. This is due to missing capability checks on multiple AJAX actions (rockpress_import, rockpress_import_status, rockpress_last_import, rockpress_reset_import, and rockpress_check_services) combined with the plugin's nonce being exposed to all authenticated users via an unconditionally enqueued admin script. The plugin enqueues the 'rockpress-admin' script on all admin pages (incl

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows any authenticated user, including those with minimal privileges, to execute sensitive AJAX actions within the RockPress plugin. This could lead to unauthorized data imports, resets, or service checks, potentially compromising the integrity and functionality of WordPress sites using the plugin.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the RockPress plugin until a patched version is released.
Restrict access to the WordPress admin panel to trusted users only and review user roles and permissions.
Monitor logs for unusual activity related to AJAX actions and implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block unauthorized requests.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: add xmit recursion...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: add xmit recursion limit to tunnel xmit functions

Tunnel xmit functions (iptunnel_xmit, ip6tunnel_xmit) lack their own
recursion limit. When a bond device in broadcast mode has GRE tap
interfaces as slaves, and those GRE tunnels route back through the
bond, multicast/broadcast traffic triggers infinite recursion between
bond_xmit_broadcast() and ip_tunnel_xmit()/ip6_tnl_xmit(), causing
kernel stack overflow.

The existing

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel can lead to a kernel stack overflow due to infinite recursion in tunnel xmit functions when specific network configurations are used. This could result in denial of service (DoS) attacks, causing system crashes or instability.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Linux kernel to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-23276.
Audit network configurations, especially those using GRE tunnels and bond devices in broadcast mode, to ensure they are not susceptible to this vulnerability.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unusual network traffic patterns that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net/sched: teql: fix NULL...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net/sched: teql: fix NULL pointer dereference in iptunnel_xmit on TEQL slave xmit

teql_master_xmit() calls netdev_start_xmit(skb, slave) to transmit
through slave devices, but does not update skb->dev to the slave device
beforehand.

When a gretap tunnel is a TEQL slave, the transmit path reaches
iptunnel_xmit() which saves dev = skb->dev (still pointing to teql0
master) and later calls iptunnel_xmit_stats(dev, pkt_len). This

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23277.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

netfilter: nf_tables:...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

netfilter: nf_tables: unconditionally bump set->nelems before insertion

In case that the set is full, a new element gets published then removed
without waiting for the RCU grace period, while RCU reader can be
walking over it already.

To address this issue, add the element transaction even if set is full,
but toggle the set_full flag to report -ENFILE so the abort path safely
unwinds the set to its previous state.

As for ele

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23272.

title: The CM Custom Reports – Flexible reporting to track what matters most plugin for WordPress is...
description: The CM Custom Reports – Flexible reporting to track what matters most plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via admin settings in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.7 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level permissions and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. This only affects multi-site installati

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with administrator-level permissions to inject malicious scripts into pages, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or further exploitation of users accessing the affected pages. This is particularly concerning for multi-site WordPress installations, as the scope of the attack could extend across multiple sites.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the CM Custom Reports plugin to the latest patched version if available or disable the plugin temporarily.
Restrict administrator-level access to trusted users only and audit admin accounts for any suspicious activity.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts and monitor for unusual behavior on multi-site installations.

title: The ilGhera Carta Docente for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Path Traversal in...
description: The ilGhera Carta Docente for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Path Traversal in all versions up to, and including, 1.5.0 via the 'cert' parameter of the 'wccd-delete-certificate' AJAX action. This is due to insufficient file path validation before performing a file deletion. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Administrator-level access and above, to delete arbitrary files on the server, such as wp-config.php, which can make site takeover and remote code ex

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2421.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

netfilter: xt_IDLETIMER:...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

netfilter: xt_IDLETIMER: reject rev0 reuse of ALARM timer labels

IDLETIMER revision 0 rules reuse existing timers by label and always call
mod_timer() on timer->timer.

If the label was created first by revision 1 with XT_IDLETIMER_ALARM,
the object uses alarm timer semantics and timer->timer is never initialized.
Reusing that object from revision 0 causes mod_timer() on an uninitialized
timer_list, triggering debugobjects war

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23274.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

macvlan: observe an RCU...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

macvlan: observe an RCU grace period in macvlan_common_newlink() error path

valis reported that a race condition still happens after my prior patch.

macvlan_common_newlink() might have made @dev visible before
detecting an error, and its caller will directly call free_netdev(dev).

We must respect an RCU period, either in macvlan or the core networking
stack.

After adding a temporary mdelay(1000) in macvlan_forward_source_on

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's macvlan functionality could allow a race condition to expose network devices prematurely, potentially leading to resource mismanagement, denial of service, or exploitation by attackers. This issue could impact systems relying on macvlan for network virtualization.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest Linux kernel patches that address CVE-2026-23273 to ensure the vulnerability is mitigated.
Conduct a review of systems utilizing macvlan to identify potential exposure and implement additional monitoring for unusual activity.
Isolate critical systems and workloads from potentially vulnerable macvlan configurations until patched.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

perf: Fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

perf: Fix __perf_event_overflow() vs perf_remove_from_context() race

Make sure that __perf_event_overflow() runs with IRQs disabled for all
possible callchains. Specifically the software events can end up running
it with only preemption disabled.

This opens up a race vs perf_event_exit_event() and friends that will go
and free various things the overflow path expects to be present, like
the BPF program.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23271.

The vendor was contacted early about thi

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

io_uring: ensure ctx->rings...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

io_uring: ensure ctx->rings is stable for task work flags manipulation

If DEFER_TASKRUN | SETUP_TASKRUN is used and task work is added while
the ring is being resized, it's possible for the OR'ing of
IORING_SQ_TASKRUN to happen in the small window of swapping into the
new rings and the old rings being freed.

Prevent this by adding a 2nd ->rings pointer, ->rings_rcu, which is
protected by RCU. The task work flags manipulation

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23275.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

netfilter: nf_tables: always...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

netfilter: nf_tables: always walk all pending catchall elements

During transaction processing we might have more than one catchall element:
1 live catchall element and 1 pending element that is coming as part of the
new batch.

If the map holding the catchall elements is also going away, its
required to toggle all catchall elements and not just the first viable
candidate.

Otherwise, we get:
 WARNING: ./include/net/netfilter/n

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's netfilter subsystem could allow improper handling of catchall elements during transaction processing, potentially leading to system instability, denial of service, or exploitation by attackers to manipulate network traffic.
recommended_actions: Update Linux kernel to the latest patched version that resolves CVE-2026-23278.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using netfilter to ensure proper configuration and mitigate potential risks.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability was found in Yi Technology YI Home Camera 2 2.1.1_20171024151200. The impacted...
description: A vulnerability was found in Yi Technology YI Home Camera 2 2.1.1_20171024151200. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file home/web/ipc of the component CGI Endpoint. Performing a manipulation results in missing authentication. Access to the local network is required for this attack. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4476.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Yi Technology YI Home Camera 2 2.1.1_20171024151200. The...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Yi Technology YI Home Camera 2 2.1.1_20171024151200. The affected element is an unknown function of the file home/web/ipc. Such manipulation leads to hard-coded credentials. Access to the local network is required for this attack to succeed. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Yi Technology YI Home Camera 2 allows attackers with local network access to exploit hard-coded credentials, potentially leading to unauthorized access, surveillance, or control of the device. This could compromise user privacy and security, as well as serve as a pivot point for further attacks on the local network.
recommended_actions: Immediately isolate affected devices from the local network until a patch or mitigation is available.
Implement network segmentation to restrict IoT devices from accessing sensitive parts of the network.
Replace or decommission affected devices if no vendor support or patch is provided.

title: CVE-2025-14811 - IBM Sterling Partner Engagement Manager 6.2.3.0 through 6.2.3.5 and 6.2.4.0 through 6.2.4.2 could al
description: IBM Sterling Partner Engagement Manager 6.2.3.0 through 6.2.3.5 and 6.2.4.0 through 6.2.4.2 could allow an attacker to obtain sensitive information from the query string of an HTTP GET method to process a request which could be obtained using man in the middle techniques.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability could allow attackers to intercept sensitive information via a man-in-the-middle attack, exploiting the query string of HTTP GET requests. This could lead to data leakage but requires specific conditions for exploitation.
recommended_actions: Implement HTTPS to encrypt all HTTP traffic and prevent interception of sensitive information.
Upgrade IBM Sterling Partner Engagement Manager to the latest patched version once available.
Conduct regular security audits to identify and mitigate vulnerabilities in data transmission.

title: CVE-2025-14504 - IBM Sterling B2B Integrator and IBM Sterling File Gateway 6.1.0.0 through 6.1.2.7_2, 6.2.0.0 through
description: IBM Sterling B2B Integrator and IBM Sterling File Gateway 6.1.0.0 through 6.1.2.7_2, 6.2.0.0 through 6.2.0.5_1, 6.2.1.0 through 6.2.1.1_1, and 6.2.2.0 is vulnerable to cross-site scripting.
